basmala: In the name of God, the All-Merciful, the Compassionate.
intro: Al-Nūr — the sūra of the Light — promulgates the ordinances of modesty and measures the gravity of slander against chaste women, whose innocence is solemnly vindicated in connection with the trial of Aïcha. It commands lowering the gaze and guarding chastity, institutes the oath of imprecation, and prescribes asking leave at the entrance of houses and drawing the veil over the bosom. At its heart shines the supreme Verse of Light: “God is the Light of the heavens and the earth; the likeness of His Light is as a niche wherein is a lamp, the lamp in a glass, the glass as a glittering star, kindled from a blessed olive tree neither of the East nor of the West, whose oil would almost glow though no fire touched it — light upon light; God guides to His Light whom He wills.” Then come the houses God has permitted to be raised, where His Name is remembered, by men whom neither trade nor commerce distracts from the remembrance of God; and the deeds of the deniers, like a mirage in a barren plain, or like darknesses heaped upon a deep sea. Qushayrī makes of this Light the light of gnosis set in the believer's heart: the breast is the niche, the heart the lamp, and gnosis the limpid oil that feeds it; and of “light upon light” the light of faith acquired by effort, laid upon the light found by sheer grace of God — evidence added to proof.
themes: lowering the gaze and guarding chastity
the mirage of deeds without faith

versets.ar: بِسْمِ اللَّهِ الرَّحْمَٰنِ الرَّحِيمِ سُورَةٌ أَنزَلْنَاهَا وَفَرَضْنَاهَا وَأَنزَلْنَا فِيهَا آيَاتٍ بَيِّنَاتٍ لَّعَلَّكُمْ تَذَكَّرُونَ
versets.tr: sūratun anzalnāhā wa-faraḍnāhā wa-anzalnā fīhā āyātin bayyinātin laʿallakum tadhakkarūna
versets.trad: This is a sūra We have sent down and made binding, in which We have revealed clear signs, that you may take heed.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: this sūra is an honor for you, O Muḥammad; We sent it down, for the least challenge ever issued was “bring one sūra” — and every sūra is thus a miracle and a proof for the Prophet. In it God clarified the lawful and the forbidden, a guidance for you and, for hearts, a healing from the opacity of unmeaning. Its signs are manifest proofs, radiant arguments, given so that one may remember and draw a lesson.

mots.def: a chapter of the Qurʾān; a mark of honor and a miracle.

mots.racine: ء ي ي / ب ي ن

versets.ar: الزَّانِيَةُ وَالزَّانِي فَاجْلِدُوا كُلَّ وَاحِدٍ مِّنْهُمَا مِائَةَ جَلْدَةٍ ۖ وَلَا تَأْخُذْكُم بِهِمَا رَأْفَةٌ فِي دِينِ اللَّهِ إِن كُنتُمْ تُؤْمِنُونَ بِاللَّهِ وَالْيَوْمِ الْآخِرِ ۖ وَلْيَشْهَدْ عَذَابَهُمَا طَائِفَةٌ مِّنَ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ
versets.tr: al-zāniyatu wa-l-zānī fa-jlidū kulla wāḥidin minhumā miʾata jaldatin wa-lā taʾkhudhkum bihimā raʾfatun fī dīni llāhi in kuntum tuʾminūna bi-llāhi wa-l-yawmi l-ākhiri wa-l-yashhad ʿadhābahumā ṭāʾifatun mina l-muʾminīna
versets.trad: The fornicatress and the fornicator: flog each of them with a hundred lashes. Let no compassion for them deter you from applying God's Law, if you believe in God and the Last Day. And let a group of believers witness their punishment.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī observes that though the punishment for fornication is severe, its proof has been made nearly impossible: no testimony is accepted without direct description of the act, which can be no easy matter. Glory to Him who made the sin grave and its establishment arduous! The Prophet himself multiplied openings for one who confessed — “perhaps you only touched?” — to ward off the penalty. What the Real commands must be met with hearing and obedience; the mercy enjoined by the Law is praiseworthy, but that which springs from mere natural inclination is blameworthy. And it is said: He forbids us pity toward them, while He Himself shows them mercy by not erasing, through this sin, the inscription of faith from their hearts.

versets.ar: الزَّانِي لَا يَنكِحُ إِلَّا زَانِيَةً أَوْ مُشْرِكَةً وَالزَّانِيَةُ لَا يَنكِحُهَا إِلَّا زَانٍ أَوْ مُشْرِكٌ ۚ وَحُرِّمَ ذَٰلِكَ عَلَى الْمُؤْمِنِينَ
versets.tr: al-zānī lā yankiḥu illā zāniyatan aw mushrikatan wa-l-zāniyatu lā yankiḥuhā illā zānin aw mushrikun wa-ḥurrima dhālika ʿalā l-muʾminīna
versets.trad: The fornicator shall marry only a fornicatress or an idolatress, and the fornicatress shall marry only a fornicator or an idolater. Such a union is forbidden to the believers.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: people go by affinities; each being dwells with its like. As the verse says: “Ask not about the man, but about his companion — for every comrade imitates the one he keeps.” The people of corruption are gathered together, however far apart their abodes, and the people of uprightness are joined, however distant their homes. Union mirrors the likeness of souls.

mots.def: mushrika: one who associates partners with God.

versets.ar: وَالَّذِينَ يَرْمُونَ الْمُحْصَنَاتِ ثُمَّ لَمْ يَأْتُوا بِأَرْبَعَةِ شُهَدَاءَ فَاجْلِدُوهُمْ ثَمَانِينَ جَلْدَةً وَلَا تَقْبَلُوا لَهُمْ شَهَادَةً أَبَدًا ۚ وَأُولَٰئِكَ هُمُ الْفَاسِقُونَ
versets.tr: wa-lladhīna yarmūna l-muḥṣanāti thumma lam yaʾtū bi-arbaʿati shuhadāʾa fa-jlidūhum thamānīna jaldatan wa-lā taqbalū lahum shahādatan abadan wa-ulāʾika humu l-fāsiqūna
versets.trad: Those who accuse chaste women and then fail to produce four witnesses: flog them with eighty lashes and never again accept their testimony — those are the depraved,
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: so that none may hold lawful the honor of the Muslims, and none may tear the veil of others, God ordained that the slanderer be chastised and the penalty applied if he brings no witnesses. He set the required number of witnesses at the highest, and the rejection of the defamer's testimony as final. In the ḥadīth: “Let whoever falls into these defilements cover himself with God's veil, for whoever uncovers his face to us, We apply upon him the penalty of God.”

mots.def: ramā: to cast; here, to defame.
fāsiq: one who departs from obedience.

versets.ar: إِلَّا الَّذِينَ تَابُوا مِن بَعْدِ ذَٰلِكَ وَأَصْلَحُوا فَإِنَّ اللَّهَ غَفُورٌ رَّحِيمٌ
versets.tr: illā lladhīna tābū min baʿdi dhālika wa-aṣlaḥū fa-inna llāha ghafūrun raḥīmun
versets.trad: except for those who repent thereafter and amend themselves. Truly God is forgiving, compassionate.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: God made the validity of repentance a condition for accepting the testimony, and amendment the sign of that sincere repentance. After his return, a span must pass in which his probity spreads as widely as his slander once spread. All this is a severity intended for whoever would guard, before the believers, the appearance of his uprightness.

mots.def: tāba: to turn back to God.

mots.racine: غ ف ر / ر ح م

versets.ar: وَالَّذِينَ يَرْمُونَ أَزْوَاجَهُمْ وَلَمْ يَكُن لَّهُمْ شُهَدَاءُ إِلَّا أَنفُسُهُمْ فَشَهَادَةُ أَحَدِهِمْ أَرْبَعُ شَهَادَاتٍ بِاللَّهِ ۙ إِنَّهُ لَمِنَ الصَّادِقِينَ
versets.tr: wa-lladhīna yarmūna azwājahum wa-lam yakun lahum shuhadāʾu illā anfusuhum fa-shahādatu aḥadihim arbaʿu shahādātin bi-llāhi innahu la-mina l-ṣādiqīna
versets.trad: And those who accuse their wives, having no witnesses but themselves — the testimony of each shall be to bear witness four times by God that he is truthful,
versets.commentaire: In the face of a husband's anguish at seeing his wife in turpitude — for the Law forbids ascribing a child to another, and to tear honor without proof is proscribed — God instituted the oath of imprecation (liʿān). Qushayrī: so grave is this matter that God made it the blade that cuts off the quarrel and the dam that holds back the reckless. And he marvels: were it not that God is powerful over all things, who among men would ever have devised such a ruling? Its source is revelation, its beginning is from God, and to Him it returns.

versets.ar: وَالْخَامِسَةُ أَنَّ لَعْنَتَ اللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ إِن كَانَ مِنَ الْكَاذِبِينَ
versets.tr: wa-l-khāmisatu anna laʿnata llāhi ʿalayhi in kāna mina l-kādhibīna
versets.trad: and a fifth time to invoke God's curse upon himself if he has spoken a lie.
versets.commentaire: The fifth oath turns God's curse upon the accuser, should he be lying. Qushayrī counts this ruling among the institutions which no human mind could have conceived without a teaching from heaven and a prophetic command: a man exposes himself to the divine curse, sealing his word with a weight that surpasses every worldly ruse.

versets.ar: وَيَدْرَأُ عَنْهَا الْعَذَابَ أَن تَشْهَدَ أَرْبَعَ شَهَادَاتٍ بِاللَّهِ ۙ إِنَّهُ لَمِنَ الْكَاذِبِينَ
versets.tr: wa-yadraʾu ʿanhā l-ʿadhāba an tashhada arbaʿa shahādātin bi-llāhi innahu la-mina l-kādhibīna
versets.trad: And the punishment shall be averted from her if she bears witness four times by God that her accuser has lied,
versets.commentaire: The wife's fourfold oath averts the penalty from her. Qushayrī sees in this reciprocity the wisdom of the Law: where doubt remains insoluble between two words, God institutes a recourse that cuts off the quarrel and entrusts to Him alone the knowledge of the truth, tearing the honor of neither party.

versets.ar: وَالْخَامِسَةَ أَنَّ غَضَبَ اللَّهِ عَلَيْهَا إِن كَانَ مِنَ الصَّادِقِينَ
versets.tr: wa-l-khāmisata anna ghaḍaba llāhi ʿalayhā in kāna mina l-ṣādiqīna
versets.trad: and a fifth time to invoke God's wrath upon herself if he has spoken the truth.
versets.commentaire: The wife's fifth oath invokes God's wrath upon herself, should the husband be truthful. Qushayrī makes the whole device a sign: none could untie such a dispute by the mere resources of reason; it is from revelation that this remedy comes, and from God that it takes its origin and its end.

versets.ar: وَلَوْلَا فَضْلُ اللَّهِ عَلَيْكُمْ وَرَحْمَتُهُ وَأَنَّ اللَّهَ تَوَّابٌ حَكِيمٌ
versets.tr: wa-lawlā faḍlu llāhi ʿalaykum wa-raḥmatuhu wa-anna llāha tawwābun ḥakīmun
versets.trad: This is the effect of God's grace and His mercy toward you, of God who accepts repentance, who is wise.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī completes the suspended phrase: were it not for God's grace, you would have remained shut within this intractable affair, finding no way out of this tangled situation. That God accepts repentance and is wise — this is the twofold clemency that unties the knot which man, alone, could never have cut.

mots.def: tawwāb: divine Name, who receives the return.

versets.ar: إِنَّ الَّذِينَ جَاءُوا بِالْإِفْكِ عُصْبَةٌ مِّنكُمْ ۚ لَا تَحْسَبُوهُ شَرًّا لَّكُم ۖ بَلْ هُوَ خَيْرٌ لَّكُمْ ۚ لِكُلِّ امْرِئٍ مِّنْهُم مَّا اكْتَسَبَ مِنَ الْإِثْمِ ۚ وَالَّذِي تَوَلَّىٰ كِبْرَهُ مِنْهُمْ لَهُ عَذَابٌ عَظِيمٌ
versets.tr: inna lladhīna jāʾū bi-l-ifki ʿuṣbatun minkum lā taḥsabūhu sharran lakum bal huwa khayrun lakum li-kulli mriʾin minhum mā ktasaba mina l-ithmi wa-lladhī tawallā kibrahu minhum lahu ʿadhābun ʿaẓīmun
versets.trad: Those who came forth with the slander are a band among you. Do not reckon it an evil for you; rather it is a good for you. Each of them shall bear the share of sin he has earned, and the one who took upon himself the greater part of it shall have a terrible punishment.
versets.commentaire: This is the affair of Aïcha and the account of the slander. Qushayrī: God exempts none from trial and affliction in love and nearness; testing is one of the firmest pillars of the path, its highest proof and truest token. And he offers this stroke: God is jealous over the hearts of His elect; when some intimacy settles between them, He brings about what turns each away from the other to bring it back to Himself. When Aïcha's innocence appeared, she said: “Praise be to God, not to you” — God had turned her heart from the Messenger back to Him, and the Messenger's heart back to Him. In times of trial God closes to His friends the eyes of insight, to bring the trial to its perfection. And “it is not an evil for you, but a good”: each bore only the share that fell to him.

mots.def: ifk: grave falsehood, a thing overturned.

versets.ar: لَّوْلَا إِذْ سَمِعْتُمُوهُ ظَنَّ الْمُؤْمِنُونَ وَالْمُؤْمِنَاتُ بِأَنفُسِهِمْ خَيْرًا وَقَالُوا هَٰذَا إِفْكٌ مُّبِينٌ
versets.tr: lawlā idh samiʿtumūhu ẓanna l-muʾminūna wa-l-muʾminātu bi-anfusihim khayran wa-qālū hādhā ifkun mubīnun
versets.trad: Why, when you heard it, did the believing men and women not think well of their own and say: “This is a manifest slander”?
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: God reproaches them for hastening to objection and loosing their tongues in evil against her, instead of turning away from what touches the Prophet's wife. Then: if only they had brought witnesses! And finding none, why did they not keep silent, restraining their tongues? Thinking well of one's brother is a duty of faith.

mots.racine: ء ف ك / ب ي ن

versets.ar: لَّوْلَا جَاءُوا عَلَيْهِ بِأَرْبَعَةِ شُهَدَاءَ ۚ فَإِذْ لَمْ يَأْتُوا بِالشُّهَدَاءِ فَأُولَٰئِكَ عِندَ اللَّهِ هُمُ الْكَاذِبُونَ
versets.tr: lawlā jāʾū ʿalayhi bi-arbaʿati shuhadāʾa fa-idh lam yaʾtū bi-l-shuhadāʾi fa-ulāʾika ʿinda llāhi humu l-kādhibūna
versets.trad: Why did they not bring four witnesses to it? Since they did not bring the witnesses, in God's sight they are the liars.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: why did they not bring four witnesses to what they alleged? And finding none, why did they not fall silent, guarding their tongues? Lacking proof, it is before God that they are the liars: the verdict of the Real falls upon the destitute accuser, not upon the slandered victim.

mots.sens: if only, why did they not

versets.ar: وَلَوْلَا فَضْلُ اللَّهِ عَلَيْكُمْ وَرَحْمَتُهُ فِي الدُّنْيَا وَالْآخِرَةِ لَمَسَّكُمْ فِي مَا أَفَضْتُمْ فِيهِ عَذَابٌ عَظِيمٌ
versets.tr: wa-lawlā faḍlu llāhi ʿalaykum wa-raḥmatuhu fī l-dunyā wa-l-ākhirati la-massakum fī mā afaḍtum fīhi ʿadhābun ʿaẓīmun
versets.trad: Had it not been for God's grace and His mercy upon you in this world and the next, a terrible punishment would have touched you for the flood of talk you let yourselves be swept into,
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: God avenges His friends as He does not avenge Himself. The disbelievers say of the Real what is repugnant to Him, yet He does not cut off their provisions nor withhold His bounties; but what touches the right of His elect — above all the right of the Messenger — that is immense in God's sight. Without His grace, in this world and the next, the punishment would have reached you for that flood into which you let yourselves be swept.

mots.sens: you poured forth, overflowed in talk
mots.def: afāḍa: to pour out in speech.

versets.ar: إِذْ تَلَقَّوْنَهُ بِأَلْسِنَتِكُمْ وَتَقُولُونَ بِأَفْوَاهِكُم مَّا لَيْسَ لَكُم بِهِ عِلْمٌ وَتَحْسَبُونَهُ هَيِّنًا وَهُوَ عِندَ اللَّهِ عَظِيمٌ
versets.tr: idh talaqqawnahu bi-alsinatikum wa-taqūlūna bi-afwāhikum mā laysa lakum bihi ʿilmun wa-taḥsabūnahu hayyinan wa-huwa ʿinda llāhi ʿaẓīmun
versets.trad: when you passed it on with your tongues and uttered with your mouths what you had no knowledge of, deeming it a trifle, while in God's sight it was momentous.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: the believer's way is never to deem small an act of obedience in concord, nor to deem light a fault in dissension: for to magnify the command is to magnify the One who commands. The people of realization look not at the act but at the One who commands it. And he adds: the least fault, which the servant regards with contempt, can ruin many states and trouble many a limpid spring; and the least obedience, which he deems negligible, can be his salvation and that of a whole world with him.

mots.sens: you pass it on, receive it from mouth to mouth

versets.ar: وَلَوْلَا إِذْ سَمِعْتُمُوهُ قُلْتُم مَّا يَكُونُ لَنَا أَن نَّتَكَلَّمَ بِهَٰذَا سُبْحَانَكَ هَٰذَا بُهْتَانٌ عَظِيمٌ
versets.tr: wa-lawlā idh samiʿtumūhu qultum mā yakūnu lanā an natakallama bi-hādhā subḥānaka hādhā buhtānun ʿaẓīmun
versets.trad: Why, when you heard it, did you not say: “It is not for us to speak of this! Glory be to You — this is a monstrous calumny”?
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: to listen to backbiting is a kind of backbiting; nay, the listener is the worst of backbiters, for by his ear the slanderer's design is accomplished. When the believer hears an evil word against the Muslims without foundation, his duty is to refute it; silence without disavowal does not suffice. If the speaker is not ashamed of his speech, the listener should not be ashamed to throw it back at him.

versets.ar: يَعِظُكُمُ اللَّهُ أَن تَعُودُوا لِمِثْلِهِ أَبَدًا إِن كُنتُم مُّؤْمِنِينَ
versets.tr: yaʿiẓukumu llāhu an taʿūdū li-mithlihi abadan in kuntum muʾminīna
versets.trad: God admonishes you never to repeat the like of it, if you are believers!
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: whoever, after this, looses his tongue against Aïcha is no believer, by the literal sense of this verse. He at once nuances it: one who did so commits a grave sin, yet does not thereby leave the faith — as one says, “if you are my brother, stand by me in hardship,” without the lapse breaking the fraternal bond. The believer's duty is to abstain from such talk.

versets.ar: وَيُبَيِّنُ اللَّهُ لَكُمُ الْآيَاتِ ۚ وَاللَّهُ عَلِيمٌ حَكِيمٌ
versets.tr: wa-yubayyinu llāhu lakumu l-āyāti wa-llāhu ʿalīmun ḥakīmun
versets.trad: God makes clear to you the signs. And God is all-knowing, wise.
versets.commentaire: God makes His signs clear, by knowledge and wisdom. Qushayrī makes the divine knowledge and wisdom the twin seal that authenticates this whole order of modesty and restraint: the Real has left no room for obscurity; He has cleared the way and lit the lamp, that whoever wishes to see clearly may feel neither weariness nor toil.

mots.racine: ع ل م / ح ك م

versets.ar: إِنَّ الَّذِينَ يُحِبُّونَ أَن تَشِيعَ الْفَاحِشَةُ فِي الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا لَهُمْ عَذَابٌ أَلِيمٌ فِي الدُّنْيَا وَالْآخِرَةِ ۚ وَاللَّهُ يَعْلَمُ وَأَنتُمْ لَا تَعْلَمُونَ
versets.tr: inna lladhīna yuḥibbūna an tashīʿa l-fāḥishatu fī lladhīna āmanū lahum ʿadhābun alīmun fī l-dunyā wa-l-ākhirati wa-llāhu yaʿlamu wa-antum lā taʿlamūna
versets.trad: Those who love that indecency should spread among the believers shall have a painful punishment in this world and the next. God knows, and you do not know!
themes: the punishment of the two worlds
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: these hold a yet more odious rank and bear a heavier burden, for they have loved the disgrace of the Muslims — whereas among the pillars of religion are the support of the believers, aid to the people of faith, and the wish for good to all. Whoever desires sedition for the Muslims is the worst of creatures; God is pleased with him in no state, and judges him unworthy of attaining the pure essence of Oneness.

versets.ar: وَلَوْلَا فَضْلُ اللَّهِ عَلَيْكُمْ وَرَحْمَتُهُ وَأَنَّ اللَّهَ رَءُوفٌ رَّحِيمٌ
versets.tr: wa-lawlā faḍlu llāhi ʿalaykum wa-raḥmatuhu wa-anna llāha raʾūfun raḥīmun
versets.trad: Had it not been for God's grace and His mercy toward you... [you would already have suffered the punishment] God is most kind, compassionate.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: God repeats “had it not been for God's grace upon you and His mercy” to show all that the beautiful preservation they enjoyed came of His favor alone. And he notes this subtle stroke: everyone witnesses the bounty of a gift and gives thanks for it; but rare is the servant who perceives the bounty of being kept from harm, and praises God for it — for the first leaves a felt trace, the second passes almost unnoticed.

mots.def: raʾūf: divine Name, full of tenderness.

versets.ar: يَا أَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا لَا تَتَّبِعُوا خُطُوَاتِ الشَّيْطَانِ ۚ وَمَن يَتَّبِعْ خُطُوَاتِ الشَّيْطَانِ فَإِنَّهُ يَأْمُرُ بِالْفَحْشَاءِ وَالْمُنكَرِ ۚ وَلَوْلَا فَضْلُ اللَّهِ عَلَيْكُمْ وَرَحْمَتُهُ مَا زَكَىٰ مِنكُم مِّنْ أَحَدٍ أَبَدًا وَلَٰكِنَّ اللَّهَ يُزَكِّي مَن يَشَاءُ ۗ وَاللَّهُ سَمِيعٌ عَلِيمٌ
versets.tr: yā ayyuhā lladhīna āmanū lā tattabiʿū khuṭuwāti l-shayṭāni wa-man yattabiʿ khuṭuwāti l-shayṭāni fa-innahu yaʾmuru bi-l-faḥshāʾi wa-l-munkari wa-lawlā faḍlu llāhi ʿalaykum wa-raḥmatuhu mā zakā minkum min aḥadin abadan wa-lākinna llāha yuzakkī man yashāʾu wa-llāhu samīʿun ʿalīmun
versets.trad: O you who believe! Do not follow the steps of Satan. Whoever follows his steps — he commands indecency and what is reprehensible. Were it not for God's grace upon you and His mercy, not one of you would ever be pure. But God purifies whom He wills. And God is all-hearing, all-knowing.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: when the heart is purified of whisperings and cleared of obsessions, the lights of good inspirations rise within it; and when the servant's moment rises yet higher, the inspirations themselves vanish, and it is the speech of the Real that manifests within it. And on “without His grace, none would ever be pure”: He brings them back, in all their states, to contemplate what the Real has bestowed — purity is from God, bounty is from God, favor is from God.

versets.ar: وَلَا يَأْتَلِ أُولُو الْفَضْلِ مِنكُمْ وَالسَّعَةِ أَن يُؤْتُوا أُولِي الْقُرْبَىٰ وَالْمَسَاكِينَ وَالْمُهَاجِرِينَ فِي سَبِيلِ اللَّهِ ۖ وَلْيَعْفُوا وَلْيَصْفَحُوا ۗ أَلَا تُحِبُّونَ أَن يَغْفِرَ اللَّهُ لَكُمْ ۗ وَاللَّهُ غَفُورٌ رَّحِيمٌ
versets.tr: wa-lā yaʾtali ulū l-faḍli minkum wa-l-saʿati an yuʾtū ulī l-qurbā wa-l-masākīna wa-l-muhājirīna fī sabīli llāhi wa-l-yaʿfū wa-l-yaṣfaḥū a-lā tuḥibbūna an yaghfira llāhu lakum wa-llāhu ghafūrun raḥīmun
versets.trad: Let not those of you endowed with grace and abundance forswear giving to their kinsfolk, the needy, and those who emigrate in the path of God. Let them pardon and forgive: do you not love that God should forgive you? And God is forgiving, compassionate.
versets.commentaire: This verse came down when Abū Bakr resolved, by a very human stirring, to stop maintaining Misṭaḥ, who had taken part in the slander. Qushayrī: God did not consent that in the Veracious One there should stir the least vein of human claim, and Abū Bakr resumed his former beneficence. Then this luminous gradation: good toward the benefactor is a recompense; toward one who does neither good nor harm, it is a favor; but toward one who has wronged you, it is chivalry and generosity (futuwwa). And he distinguishes: pardon (ʿafw) bears on acts, indulgence (ṣafḥ) on the offenses of the heart.

mots.sens: let him not swear to cease, not forswear
those endowed with grace, with merit

versets.ar: إِنَّ الَّذِينَ يَرْمُونَ الْمُحْصَنَاتِ الْغَافِلَاتِ الْمُؤْمِنَاتِ لُعِنُوا فِي الدُّنْيَا وَالْآخِرَةِ وَلَهُمْ عَذَابٌ عَظِيمٌ
versets.tr: inna lladhīna yarmūna l-muḥṣanāti l-ghāfilāti l-muʾmināti luʿinū fī l-dunyā wa-l-ākhirati wa-lahum ʿadhābun ʿaẓīmun
versets.trad: Those who slander chaste, unsuspecting, believing women are cursed in this world and the next; a terrible punishment awaits them.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: the description “unsuspecting” (ghāfilāt) is no blame, but the indication of their utter strangeness to what is imputed to them — their hearts did not so much as conceive of such a fault. And the curse incurred in both worlds shows that by the baleful effect of their fault, the slanderer's destiny is overturned, to the point that he may leave this world outside of Islam.

versets.ar: يَوْمَ تَشْهَدُ عَلَيْهِمْ أَلْسِنَتُهُمْ وَأَيْدِيهِمْ وَأَرْجُلُهُم بِمَا كَانُوا يَعْمَلُونَ
versets.tr: yawma tashhadu ʿalayhim alsinatuhum wa-aydīhim wa-arjuluhum bimā kānū yaʿmalūna
versets.trad: On the Day when their tongues, their hands, and their feet shall bear witness against them concerning what they did,
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: their limbs shall bear witness against them for what they did, without their consent; but just as some limbs testify against them, others testify for them — the eye that says “he looked through me” also says “he wept through me.” And he adds, splendidly: the witness of the limbs on the Last Day is deferred, but their witness in love is, even now, present: the pallor of the face when the Beloved appears, the wan complexion, the wasting, the streaming of tears, the throbbing of the heart.

versets.ar: يَوْمَئِذٍ يُوَفِّيهِمُ اللَّهُ دِينَهُمُ الْحَقَّ وَيَعْلَمُونَ أَنَّ اللَّهَ هُوَ الْحَقُّ الْمُبِينُ
versets.tr: yawmaʾidhin yuwaffīhimu llāhu dīnahumu l-ḥaqqa wa-yaʿlamūna anna llāha huwa l-ḥaqqu l-mubīnu
versets.trad: on that Day God will pay them their just due in full, and they will know that God is the manifest Truth.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: He requites them according to their due — to the worshippers the Garden and reward for the discharge of their works; to the knowers union and nearness for the purification of their states. To some the elevation of degrees, to others the intimacy of the high contemplations and the permanence of the secret colloquy. And “they will know that God is the manifest Truth”: knowledge becomes for them a necessity, the heart rests from the effort of its wavering, grown too rich in its inner sight to need to seek any longer.

mots.def: dīn: here, the exact reckoning of deeds.

mots.racine: ح ق ق / ب ي ن

versets.ar: الْخَبِيثَاتُ لِلْخَبِيثِينَ وَالْخَبِيثُونَ لِلْخَبِيثَاتِ ۖ وَالطَّيِّبَاتُ لِلطَّيِّبِينَ وَالطَّيِّبُونَ لِلطَّيِّبَاتِ ۚ أُولَٰئِكَ مُبَرَّءُونَ مِمَّا يَقُولُونَ ۖ لَهُم مَّغْفِرَةٌ وَرِزْقٌ كَرِيمٌ
versets.tr: al-khabīthātu li-l-khabīthīna wa-l-khabīthūna li-l-khabīthāti wa-l-ṭayyibātu li-l-ṭayyibīna wa-l-ṭayyibūna li-l-ṭayyibāti ulāʾika mubarraʾūna mimmā yaqūlūna lahum maghfiratun wa-rizqun karīmun
versets.trad: Vile women are for vile men, and vile men for vile women; and good women are for good men, and good men for good women. These are declared innocent of what is said against them. For them is forgiveness and a noble provision.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī unfolds this verse in degrees. Impurities — acts, states, goods, persons — are for the impure who seek them; each is bound to what suits it, the act befits its doer and the doer his act, in purity as in defilement. And the good things for the good: the good states are the realization of union, pure of all self-interest, for the men whose aspiration has risen above all that is base — souls that reach for the heights, whose adornment is to humble themselves before Him to whom belongs all might. “Forgiveness and noble provision”: forgiveness for the hereafter, and noble provision even now — what comes to them without watching, without craving demand, without the humiliation of having to ask, without toil to outrun.

mots.racine: ر ز ق / ك ر م

versets.ar: يَا أَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا لَا تَدْخُلُوا بُيُوتًا غَيْرَ بُيُوتِكُمْ حَتَّىٰ تَسْتَأْنِسُوا وَتُسَلِّمُوا عَلَىٰ أَهْلِهَا ۚ ذَٰلِكُمْ خَيْرٌ لَّكُمْ لَعَلَّكُمْ تَذَكَّرُونَ
versets.tr: yā ayyuhā lladhīna āmanū lā tadkhulū buyūtan ghayra buyūtikum ḥattā tastaʾnisū wa-tusallimū ʿalā ahlihā dhālikum khayrun lakum laʿallakum tadhakkarūna
versets.trad: O you who believe! Do not enter houses other than your own until you have announced yourselves and greeted their occupants. That is better for you; perhaps you will take heed.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī raises the rule to the Path: the elect claim no possession they hold for themselves alone; whoever opens to them access to anything meets from them neither refusal nor rebuff. But as for what is in others' hands, they covet nothing, beg nothing, impose nothing — out of modesty before God. And he cites: “I am ashamed before God to ride a miser's mount and to beg a base man for his camel, when my Lord's camels are without number upon the earth.”

mots.def: istaʾnasa: to make oneself known, ask leave.
sallama: to offer the greeting of peace.

versets.ar: فَإِن لَّمْ تَجِدُوا فِيهَا أَحَدًا فَلَا تَدْخُلُوهَا حَتَّىٰ يُؤْذَنَ لَكُمْ ۖ وَإِن قِيلَ لَكُمُ ارْجِعُوا فَارْجِعُوا ۖ هُوَ أَزْكَىٰ لَكُمْ ۚ وَاللَّهُ بِمَا تَعْمَلُونَ عَلِيمٌ
versets.tr: fa-in lam tajidū fīhā aḥadan fa-lā tadkhulūhā ḥattā yuʾdhana lakum wa-in qīla lakumu rjiʿū fa-rjiʿū huwa azkā lakum wa-llāhu bimā taʿmalūna ʿalīmun
versets.trad: If you find no one in them, do not enter until permission is granted you. And if you are told, “Go back,” then go back: that is purer for you. And God knows all that you do.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: in this is the safeguarding of God's command and the respect of the householder's inviolability; for whoever enters without permission may uncover a nakedness or a secret the master wishes to reveal to no one. And if you are told “go back,” go back: the excuses may be real, and the possessor of a thing has the better right over his own.

versets.ar: لَّيْسَ عَلَيْكُمْ جُنَاحٌ أَن تَدْخُلُوا بُيُوتًا غَيْرَ مَسْكُونَةٍ فِيهَا مَتَاعٌ لَّكُمْ ۚ وَاللَّهُ يَعْلَمُ مَا تُبْدُونَ وَمَا تَكْتُمُونَ
versets.tr: laysa ʿalaykum junāḥun an tadkhulū buyūtan ghayra maskūnatin fīhā matāʿun lakum wa-llāhu yaʿlamu mā tubdūna wa-mā taktumūna
versets.trad: There is no blame upon you for entering uninhabited houses in which you have some convenience. And God knows what you reveal and what you conceal.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: God has lifted all blame for the use of what does not harm its owner — entering where one has an interest with no other way, so long as no damage results; like taking shade by a wall or looking into a mirror fixed in another's wall. And all this is made lawful only by the Law, not by mere reasoning, as some imagine.

versets.ar: قُل لِّلْمُؤْمِنِينَ يَغُضُّوا مِنْ أَبْصَارِهِمْ وَيَحْفَظُوا فُرُوجَهُمْ ۚ ذَٰلِكَ أَزْكَىٰ لَهُمْ ۗ إِنَّ اللَّهَ خَبِيرٌ بِمَا يَصْنَعُونَ
versets.tr: qul li-l-muʾminīna yaghuḍḍū min abṣārihim wa-yaḥfaẓū furūjahum dhālika azkā lahum inna llāha khabīrun bimā yaṣnaʿūna
versets.trad: Tell the believing men to lower their gaze and guard their chastity. That is purer for them. Truly God is well aware of what they do.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī opens the meaning: “let them lower” the outer gaze from the forbidden, the gaze of hearts from base thoughts, and their imagination from what cannot be seen. And he warns: the eye is the cause of ruin; “whoever looses his gaze hastens his death.” The devil says: “the gaze is my ancient bow, my arrow that does not miss.” So the men of spiritual struggle, wishing to guard their hearts from evil promptings, would not look upon sensory things: this is a great principle of their combat in ascesis.

versets.ar: وَقُل لِّلْمُؤْمِنَاتِ يَغْضُضْنَ مِنْ أَبْصَارِهِنَّ وَيَحْفَظْنَ فُرُوجَهُنَّ وَلَا يُبْدِينَ زِينَتَهُنَّ إِلَّا مَا ظَهَرَ مِنْهَا ۖ وَلْيَضْرِبْنَ بِخُمُرِهِنَّ عَلَىٰ جُيُوبِهِنَّ ۖ وَلَا يُبْدِينَ زِينَتَهُنَّ إِلَّا لِبُعُولَتِهِنَّ أَوْ آبَائِهِنَّ أَوْ آبَاءِ بُعُولَتِهِنَّ أَوْ أَبْنَائِهِنَّ أَوْ أَبْنَاءِ بُعُولَتِهِنَّ أَوْ إِخْوَانِهِنَّ أَوْ بَنِي إِخْوَانِهِنَّ أَوْ بَنِي أَخَوَاتِهِنَّ أَوْ نِسَائِهِنَّ أَوْ مَا مَلَكَتْ أَيْمَانُهُنَّ أَوِ التَّابِعِينَ غَيْرِ أُولِي الْإِرْبَةِ مِنَ الرِّجَالِ أَوِ الطِّفْلِ الَّذِينَ لَمْ يَظْهَرُوا عَلَىٰ عَوْرَاتِ النِّسَاءِ ۖ وَلَا يَضْرِبْنَ بِأَرْجُلِهِنَّ لِيُعْلَمَ مَا يُخْفِينَ مِن زِينَتِهِنَّ ۚ وَتُوبُوا إِلَى اللَّهِ جَمِيعًا أَيُّهَ الْمُؤْمِنُونَ لَعَلَّكُمْ تُفْلِحُونَ
versets.tr: wa-qul li-l-muʾmināti yaghḍuḍna min abṣārihinna wa-yaḥfaẓna furūjahunna wa-lā yubdīna zīnatahunna illā mā ẓahara minhā wa-l-yaḍribna bi-khumurihinna ʿalā juyūbihinna wa-lā yubdīna zīnatahunna illā li-buʿūlatihinna aw ābāʾihinna aw ābāʾi buʿūlatihinna aw abnāʾihinna aw abnāʾi buʿūlatihinna aw ikhwānihinna aw banī ikhwānihinna aw banī akhawātihinna aw nisāʾihinna aw mā malakat aymānuhunna awi l-tābiʿīna ghayri ulī l-irbati mina l-rijāli awi l-ṭifli lladhīna lam yaẓharū ʿalā ʿawrāti l-nisāʾi wa-lā yaḍribna bi-arjulihinna li-yuʿlama mā yukhfīna min zīnatihinna wa-tūbū ilā llāhi jamīʿan ayyuha l-muʾminūna laʿallakum tufliḥūna
versets.trad: And tell the believing women to lower their gaze, to guard their chastity, to display of their adornment only what necessarily appears, to draw their veils over their bosoms, and to display their adornment only to their husbands, or their fathers, or their husbands' fathers, or their sons, or their husbands' sons, or their brothers, or their brothers' sons, or their sisters' sons, or their women, or those their right hands possess, or such male attendants as have no sexual desire, or children not yet aware of women's nakedness; and not to stamp their feet so as to make known the adornment they conceal. And turn all together to God in repentance, O believers, that you may prosper.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: the obligation falls upon them as upon the men, for the spiritual charge embraces both sexes; and if they rise above this state, it is the heart's blindness to all but the Adored One. He extends the metaphor of the veil: just as a woman has an intimacy she may not unveil, so too one who displays to people the adornment of his inmost self — the purity of his states, the radiance of his works — turns that adornment into ugliness, unless something appears without his willing or seeking it. On “turn all to God”: there is repentance from faults, which is that of the common; and repentance from heedlessness, which is that of the elect — the return from the sight of the act to the sight of the grace that makes it possible, and from grace to the contemplation of the One who grants it.

mots.def: jayb: opening of the garment, breast.

versets.ar: وَأَنكِحُوا الْأَيَامَىٰ مِنكُمْ وَالصَّالِحِينَ مِنْ عِبَادِكُمْ وَإِمَائِكُمْ ۚ إِن يَكُونُوا فُقَرَاءَ يُغْنِهِمُ اللَّهُ مِن فَضْلِهِ ۗ وَاللَّهُ وَاسِعٌ عَلِيمٌ
versets.tr: wa-ankiḥū l-ayāmā minkum wa-l-ṣāliḥīna min ʿibādikum wa-imāʾikum in yakūnū fuqarāʾa yughnihimu llāhu min faḍlihi wa-llāhu wāsiʿun ʿalīmun
versets.trad: Marry off the single among you and the righteous among your male and female servants. If they are poor, God will enrich them from His grace. And God is all-embracing, all-knowing.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: if in marriage one aims at observing the proprieties of the Law, God, through His blessings, suffices for the demands of the soul and of nature; the intention must go to chastity, then to the hope of offspring who fulfill God's right. And on “if they are poor, God will enrich them”: God enriches them even now — first in person, then with the richness of the heart; and the richness of the heart is to do without the thing itself, for to be rich without the world is more perfect than to be rich through the world.

mots.def: ayyim: a person without a spouse.
aghnā: to make rich, to suffice.
wāsiʿ: divine Name, of infinite amplitude.

versets.ar: وَلْيَسْتَعْفِفِ الَّذِينَ لَا يَجِدُونَ نِكَاحًا حَتَّىٰ يُغْنِيَهُمُ اللَّهُ مِن فَضْلِهِ ۗ وَالَّذِينَ يَبْتَغُونَ الْكِتَابَ مِمَّا مَلَكَتْ أَيْمَانُكُمْ فَكَاتِبُوهُمْ إِنْ عَلِمْتُمْ فِيهِمْ خَيْرًا ۖ وَآتُوهُم مِّن مَّالِ اللَّهِ الَّذِي آتَاكُمْ ۚ وَلَا تُكْرِهُوا فَتَيَاتِكُمْ عَلَى الْبِغَاءِ إِنْ أَرَدْنَ تَحَصُّنًا لِّتَبْتَغُوا عَرَضَ الْحَيَاةِ الدُّنْيَا ۚ وَمَن يُكْرِههُّنَّ فَإِنَّ اللَّهَ مِن بَعْدِ إِكْرَاهِهِنَّ غَفُورٌ رَّحِيمٌ
versets.tr: wa-l-yastaʿfifi lladhīna lā yajidūna nikāḥan ḥattā yughniyahumu llāhu min faḍlihi wa-lladhīna yabtaghūna l-kitāba mimmā malakat aymānukum fa-kātibūhum in ʿalimtum fīhim khayran wa-ātūhum min māli llāhi lladhī ātākum wa-lā tukrihū fatayātikum ʿalā l-bighāʾi in aradna taḥaṣṣunan li-tabtaghū ʿaraḍa l-ḥayāti l-dunyā wa-man yukrihhunna fa-inna llāha min baʿdi ikrāhihinna ghafūrun raḥīmun
versets.trad: Let those who find no means to marry keep chaste until God enriches them from His grace. And for those of your servants who seek a deed of emancipation, draw it up for them if you know some good in them, and give them of the wealth of God which He has given you. Do not force your slave-girls into prostitution, when they desire to remain chaste, in order to seek the goods of the present life. Whoever forces them — then God, after their being compelled, is forgiving, compassionate.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī makes the deed of emancipation a parable of the soul: the worshipper is like the slave who buys himself back from his Lord by graded installments, striving to free his neck, in dread of remaining in the bonds of servitude and in desire to open the door of freedom. And he offers this word of the ḥadīth: “the slave under contract remains a slave so long as a single dirham is owed” — thus the servant is not truly free while there remains in him a remnant of self-will, of attachment to other-than-God: while one dirham remains, he is in full servitude.

mots.sens: chastity, the wish to keep oneself

versets.ar: وَلَقَدْ أَنزَلْنَا إِلَيْكُمْ آيَاتٍ مُّبَيِّنَاتٍ وَمَثَلًا مِّنَ الَّذِينَ خَلَوْا مِن قَبْلِكُمْ وَمَوْعِظَةً لِّلْمُتَّقِينَ
versets.tr: wa-laqad anzalnā ilaykum āyātin mubayyinātin wa-mathalan mina lladhīna khalaw min qablikum wa-mawʿiẓatan li-l-muttaqīna
versets.trad: And We have indeed sent down to you clarifying signs, an example of those who passed away before you, and an admonition for the God-fearing.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: God has left upon the path of proof no speck of dust, nor given any place to perplexity; rather He has cleared the road, lit the lamp, illumined the way, and made the guide shine. So whoever wishes to see clearly feels neither weariness nor toil: the clarity is wholly on God's side, the blindness on the side of whoever turns away.

versets.ar: اللَّهُ نُورُ السَّمَاوَاتِ وَالْأَرْضِ ۚ مَثَلُ نُورِهِ كَمِشْكَاةٍ فِيهَا مِصْبَاحٌ ۖ الْمِصْبَاحُ فِي زُجَاجَةٍ ۖ الزُّجَاجَةُ كَأَنَّهَا كَوْكَبٌ دُرِّيٌّ يُوقَدُ مِن شَجَرَةٍ مُّبَارَكَةٍ زَيْتُونَةٍ لَّا شَرْقِيَّةٍ وَلَا غَرْبِيَّةٍ يَكَادُ زَيْتُهَا يُضِيءُ وَلَوْ لَمْ تَمْسَسْهُ نَارٌ ۚ نُّورٌ عَلَىٰ نُورٍ ۗ يَهْدِي اللَّهُ لِنُورِهِ مَن يَشَاءُ ۚ وَيَضْرِبُ اللَّهُ الْأَمْثَالَ لِلنَّاسِ ۗ وَاللَّهُ بِكُلِّ شَيْءٍ عَلِيمٌ
versets.tr: allāhu nūru l-samāwāti wa-l-arḍi mathalu nūrihi ka-mishkātin fīhā miṣbāḥun al-miṣbāḥu fī zujājatin al-zujājatu ka-annahā kawkabun durriyyun yūqadu min shajaratin mubārakatin zaytūnatin lā sharqiyyatin wa-lā gharbiyyatin yakādu zaytuhā yuḍīʾu wa-law lam tamsashu nārun nūrun ʿalā nūrin yahdī llāhu li-nūrihi man yashāʾu wa-yaḍribu llāhu l-amthāla li-l-nāsi wa-llāhu bi-kulli shayʾin ʿalīmun
versets.trad: God is the Light of the heavens and the earth. The likeness of His Light is as a niche wherein is a lamp; the lamp in a glass, the glass as it were a glittering star, kindled from a blessed tree, an olive neither of the East nor of the West, whose oil would almost glow though no fire touched it — light upon light. God guides to His Light whom He wills. And God strikes parables for mankind. And God is the Knower of all things.
versets.commentaire: The heart of the sūra. Qushayrī: God adorns the heavens with stars, and likewise He adorns hearts with lights — the light of reason, of intellect, of knowledge, of certainty, of gnosis, of Oneness; each casts its ray according to its measure. Then he unveils the parable: this is the light of the believer's heart, which is his gnosis: the breast is the niche, the heart in the breast is the lamp in the niche, and the lamp — the heart — is the glittering star; the limpid oil that feeds it is gnosis. On “light upon light”: a light acquired by their effort, their reflection, their inquiry, and a light found by God's grace — evidence added to proof, vision added to exposition: light upon light. On “neither of the East nor of the West”: their fear is never severed from hope to the point of nearing despair, nor their hope from fear to the point of nearing false security; the two stay in balance — majesty against intimacy, contraction against expansion, observance of the Law against realization of the Truth.

mots.def: nūr: light; here, Guide and Illuminator of heaven and earth.
mishkāt: a wall-niche where a lamp is set.

mots.racine: ك و ك ب / د ر ر

mots.def: the superposition of the lights of faith and of innate disposition.

versets.ar: فِي بُيُوتٍ أَذِنَ اللَّهُ أَن تُرْفَعَ وَيُذْكَرَ فِيهَا اسْمُهُ يُسَبِّحُ لَهُ فِيهَا بِالْغُدُوِّ وَالْآصَالِ
versets.tr: fī buyūtin adhina llāhu an turfaʿa wa-yudhkara fīhā smuhu yusabbiḥu lahu fīhā bi-l-ghuduwwi wa-l-āṣāli
versets.trad: In houses which God has permitted to be raised, and in which His Name is remembered, He is glorified within them, morning and evening,
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: the mosques are the houses of God, and He has permitted that needs be raised up in them to Him, that He may grant them. Then he interiorizes: the mosques are the houses of worship, and the hearts the houses of aspiration; the worshipper reaches by his worship the reward of God, and the aspirant reaches by his desire God Himself. And deeper still: the hearts are the houses of gnosis, the spirits the theaters of love, and the secrets the places of contemplation.

mots.def: bayt: house; here, mosques and hearts.

mots.racine: غ د و / ء ص ل

versets.ar: رِجَالٌ لَّا تُلْهِيهِمْ تِجَارَةٌ وَلَا بَيْعٌ عَن ذِكْرِ اللَّهِ وَإِقَامِ الصَّلَاةِ وَإِيتَاءِ الزَّكَاةِ ۙ يَخَافُونَ يَوْمًا تَتَقَلَّبُ فِيهِ الْقُلُوبُ وَالْأَبْصَارُ
versets.tr: rijālun lā tulhīhim tijāratun wa-lā bayʿun ʿan dhikri llāhi wa-iqāmi l-ṣalāti wa-ītāʾi l-zakāti yakhāfūna yawman tataqallabu fīhi l-qulūbu wa-l-abṣāru
versets.trad: men whom neither trade nor commerce distracts from the remembrance of God, the performance of prayer, and the giving of alms. They fear a Day when hearts and eyes will be overturned.
themes: the trade that does not distract
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī notes the fineness of the wording: He did not say “they do not trade or sell,” but “neither trade nor commerce distracts them from the remembrance of God”; to join the two is possible — yet nearly impossible, save for the greatest, over whom affairs pass without snatching them from Him. They are those who prefer the rights of the Real to the portions of the soul; at the muezzin's call, they leave trade and sale to render Him His due.

mots.sens: will be overturned, will turn about

versets.ar: لِيَجْزِيَهُمُ اللَّهُ أَحْسَنَ مَا عَمِلُوا وَيَزِيدَهُم مِّن فَضْلِهِ ۗ وَاللَّهُ يَرْزُقُ مَن يَشَاءُ بِغَيْرِ حِسَابٍ
versets.tr: li-yajziyahumu llāhu aḥsana mā ʿamilū wa-yazīdahum min faḍlihi wa-llāhu yarzuqu man yashāʾu bi-ghayri ḥisābin
versets.trad: that God may reward them for the best of what they did and increase them of His grace. And God provides for whom He wills without measure.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: whoever frees himself from reckoning midway sees the reckoning fall away with him; but whoever remains captive to his claims — for him the balance that Day will be the Truth itself. And provision “without measure” concerns the provisions of the spirits — for those of the bodies are measured and numbered, being fleeting portions; whereas the unveiling of the spirits through the contemplation of Beauty and Majesty is without end.

versets.ar: وَالَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا أَعْمَالُهُمْ كَسَرَابٍ بِقِيعَةٍ يَحْسَبُهُ الظَّمْآنُ مَاءً حَتَّىٰ إِذَا جَاءَهُ لَمْ يَجِدْهُ شَيْئًا وَوَجَدَ اللَّهَ عِندَهُ فَوَفَّاهُ حِسَابَهُ ۗ وَاللَّهُ سَرِيعُ الْحِسَابِ
versets.tr: wa-lladhīna kafarū aʿmāluhum ka-sarābin bi-qīʿatin yaḥsabuhu l-ẓamʾānu māʾan ḥattā idhā jāʾahu lam yajidhu shayʾan wa-wajada llāha ʿindahu fa-waffāhu ḥisābahu wa-llāhu sarīʿu l-ḥisābi
versets.trad: As for those who disbelieve, their deeds are like a mirage in a barren plain: the thirsty man supposes it to be water, until, when he comes to it, he finds it to be nothing, and finds God there, who pays him his account in full. And God is swift in reckoning.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī recalls “they suppose they are doing good” and “they imagine they hold to something.” Whoever takes the mirage for drink does not tarry long before he knows it was illusion: the thirst redoubles, and the spirit cries out for rescue. Deeds void of faith are this deceiving mirage: they promise water and yield only sand, and at the end it is God whom one finds, settling the account without delay.

mots.def: sarāb: illusion of water in the desert.

versets.ar: أَوْ كَظُلُمَاتٍ فِي بَحْرٍ لُّجِّيٍّ يَغْشَاهُ مَوْجٌ مِّن فَوْقِهِ مَوْجٌ مِّن فَوْقِهِ سَحَابٌ ۚ ظُلُمَاتٌ بَعْضُهَا فَوْقَ بَعْضٍ إِذَا أَخْرَجَ يَدَهُ لَمْ يَكَدْ يَرَاهَا ۗ وَمَن لَّمْ يَجْعَلِ اللَّهُ لَهُ نُورًا فَمَا لَهُ مِن نُّورٍ
versets.tr: aw ka-ẓulumātin fī baḥrin lujjiyyin yaghshāhu mawjun min fawqihi mawjun min fawqihi saḥābun ẓulumātun baʿḍuhā fawqa baʿḍin idhā akhraja yadahu lam yakad yarāhā wa-man lam yajʿali llāhu lahu nūran fa-mā lahu min nūrin
versets.trad: Or like darknesses upon a deep sea: a wave covers it, above which is a wave, above which are clouds — darknesses heaped one upon another. When he stretches out his hand he can hardly see it. And he to whom God gives no light has no light at all.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: the darknesses of false opinion, the clouds of dispersion, the nights of denial, the gloom of doubt — when they heap together, their prisoner has no lamp, no star, no moon, no sun. Woe, and woe again! And on “he to whom God gives no light”: if the servant has not received as his portion the light of the decree, nor the aid of a bond of election, his effort and toil remain barren, bereft of their fruits; for beginnings prevail over endings, and acceptance, for one worthy of it, is not won by means alone.

mots.racine: ب ح ر / ل ج ج

versets.ar: أَلَمْ تَرَ أَنَّ اللَّهَ يُسَبِّحُ لَهُ مَن فِي السَّمَاوَاتِ وَالْأَرْضِ وَالطَّيْرُ صَافَّاتٍ ۖ كُلٌّ قَدْ عَلِمَ صَلَاتَهُ وَتَسْبِيحَهُ ۗ وَاللَّهُ عَلِيمٌ بِمَا يَفْعَلُونَ
versets.tr: a-lam tara anna llāha yusabbiḥu lahu man fī l-samāwāti wa-l-arḍi wa-l-ṭayru ṣāffātin kullun qad ʿalima ṣalātahu wa-tasbīḥahu wa-llāhu ʿalīmun bimā yafʿalūna
versets.trad: Have you not seen that all who are in the heavens and the earth glorify God, and the birds with wings outspread? Each knows its own prayer and glorification. And God is aware of what they do.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī distinguishes two glorifications: the glorification of speech and utterance, and the glorification of indication and creation. The second is universal, of every creature, trace, or imprint; the first is proper to those endowed with reason, and divides again: a glorification born of insight — accepted — and a glorification void of knowledge — rejected. Thus praise has its rank according to the light that inhabits it.

mots.def: ṣāffa: that spreads its wings in ranks.

versets.ar: وَلِلَّهِ مُلْكُ السَّمَاوَاتِ وَالْأَرْضِ ۖ وَإِلَى اللَّهِ الْمَصِيرُ
versets.tr: wa-li-llāhi mulku l-samāwāti wa-l-arḍi wa-ilā llāhi l-maṣīru
versets.trad: To God belongs the dominion of the heavens and the earth, and to God is the return.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: dominion (mulk) is the intensity of possession, and possession is the power to bring into being: the possibles, before they even are, belong to the Creator; and in the moment of their coming-to-be after nothingness, they return to what they were. His dominion neither arises nor passes away, and nothing of it tends toward annihilation.

versets.ar: أَلَمْ تَرَ أَنَّ اللَّهَ يُزْجِي سَحَابًا ثُمَّ يُؤَلِّفُ بَيْنَهُ ثُمَّ يَجْعَلُهُ رُكَامًا فَتَرَى الْوَدْقَ يَخْرُجُ مِنْ خِلَالِهِ وَيُنَزِّلُ مِنَ السَّمَاءِ مِن جِبَالٍ فِيهَا مِن بَرَدٍ فَيُصِيبُ بِهِ مَن يَشَاءُ وَيَصْرِفُهُ عَن مَّن يَشَاءُ ۖ يَكَادُ سَنَا بَرْقِهِ يَذْهَبُ بِالْأَبْصَارِ
versets.tr: a-lam tara anna llāha yuzjī saḥāban thumma yuʾallifu baynahu thumma yajʿaluhu rukāman fa-tarā l-wadqa yakhruju min khilālihi wa-yunazzilu mina l-samāʾi min jibālin fīhā min baradin fa-yuṣību bihi man yashāʾu wa-yaṣrifuhu ʿan man yashāʾu yakādu sanā barqihi yadhhabu bi-l-abṣāri
versets.trad: Have you not seen that God drives the clouds, then joins them, then heaps them up, and you see the rain issue from their midst? From the sky He sends down mountains [of clouds] wherein is hail, and He strikes with it whom He wills and turns it from whom He wills. The flash of His lightning nearly snatches away the sight.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: God makes Himself known to the hearts of the learned through the tokens of His handiwork, in the wonder of His wisdom, and through what therein shows of the perfection of His power and the breadth of His knowledge. By His power He raises the vapors of the sea, drives them where He wills, turns to sweet water what was not, and pours it drop by drop in a measure known to Him alone: no effort of creatures holds it back or sends it down, no cunning draws the rain to a land He does not will to water.

mots.racine: س ن و / ب ر ق

versets.ar: يُقَلِّبُ اللَّهُ اللَّيْلَ وَالنَّهَارَ ۚ إِنَّ فِي ذَٰلِكَ لَعِبْرَةً لِّأُولِي الْأَبْصَارِ
versets.tr: yuqallibu llāhu l-layla wa-l-nahāra inna fī dhālika la-ʿibratan li-ulī l-abṣāri
versets.trad: God alternates the night and the day. Surely in that is a lesson for those who have sight.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: God alternates the night and the day — and likewise He turns about all other-than-Him, all vestiges and traces: such is the ordaining of the Mighty, the Knowing. Whoever scrutinizes this turning with the inner eye finds in it the coolness of certainty; whoever turns away remains in the hollow of denial and the darknesses of ignorance.

mots.racine: ل ي ل / ن ه ر

versets.ar: وَاللَّهُ خَلَقَ كُلَّ دَابَّةٍ مِّن مَّاءٍ ۖ فَمِنْهُم مَّن يَمْشِي عَلَىٰ بَطْنِهِ وَمِنْهُم مَّن يَمْشِي عَلَىٰ رِجْلَيْنِ وَمِنْهُم مَّن يَمْشِي عَلَىٰ أَرْبَعٍ ۚ يَخْلُقُ اللَّهُ مَا يَشَاءُ ۚ إِنَّ اللَّهَ عَلَىٰ كُلِّ شَيْءٍ قَدِيرٌ
versets.tr: wa-llāhu khalaqa kulla dābbatin min māʾin fa-minhum man yamshī ʿalā baṭnihi wa-minhum man yamshī ʿalā rijlayni wa-minhum man yamshī ʿalā arbaʿin yakhluqu llāhu mā yashāʾu inna llāha ʿalā kulli shayʾin qadīrun
versets.trad: And God created every living creature from water. Among them are those that crawl upon their bellies, those that walk on two legs, and those that walk on four. God creates what He wills. Truly God is powerful over all things.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī meditates on the oneness of origin and the diversity of forms: every creature is created from a water whose parts are at first equal and alike; then it divides into outward and inward members, each organ distinguished by its figure, its form, its structure. Thence feather, wool, hair, hoof, claw; flesh, fat, skin, bone, nerve, vein. To consider this with the eye of the lesson imposes the prostration of insight and the strength of attainment.

mots.def: māʾ: water, the principle of life.

versets.ar: لَّقَدْ أَنزَلْنَا آيَاتٍ مُّبَيِّنَاتٍ ۚ وَاللَّهُ يَهْدِي مَن يَشَاءُ إِلَىٰ صِرَاطٍ مُّسْتَقِيمٍ
versets.tr: laqad anzalnā āyātin mubayyinātin wa-llāhu yahdī man yashāʾu ilā ṣirāṭin mustaqīmin
versets.trad: We have indeed sent down clarifying signs. And God guides whom He wills to a straight path.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: the signs are clear, but God guides one people to them and veils another; and the one whose sight is blocked — what use to him is the rising of the sun and the stars? Likewise, the one whose insight is sealed — what use to him are the testimonies of the sciences and the proofs of the intellects? “What use is his pupil to the son of this world, when for him lights and darknesses are all one?”

mots.racine: ص ر ط / ق و م

versets.ar: وَيَقُولُونَ آمَنَّا بِاللَّهِ وَبِالرَّسُولِ وَأَطَعْنَا ثُمَّ يَتَوَلَّىٰ فَرِيقٌ مِّنْهُم مِّن بَعْدِ ذَٰلِكَ ۚ وَمَا أُولَٰئِكَ بِالْمُؤْمِنِينَ
versets.tr: wa-yaqūlūna āmannā bi-llāhi wa-bi-l-rasūli wa-aṭaʿnā thumma yatawallā farīqun minhum min baʿdi dhālika wa-mā ulāʾika bi-l-muʾminīna
versets.trad: They say, “We believe in God and the Messenger, and we obey.” Then a group of them turns away after that: those are not believers.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: they submit outwardly and confess with the tongue; then the sincere one abides in his truthfulness, while the one who spoke only out of fear of the Muslims' sword, or for some other corrupt aim, turns away thereafter and inclines to the side of the deniers. The faith of the tongue, without the heart, does not hold to the trial of the turning away.

versets.ar: وَإِذَا دُعُوا إِلَى اللَّهِ وَرَسُولِهِ لِيَحْكُمَ بَيْنَهُمْ إِذَا فَرِيقٌ مِّنْهُم مُّعْرِضُونَ
versets.tr: wa-idhā duʿū ilā llāhi wa-rasūlihi li-yaḥkuma baynahum idhā farīqun minhum muʿriḍūna
versets.trad: And when they are called to God and His Messenger that he may judge between them, behold, a group of them turns away.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: they know that their disgrace lies in their intention; whoever knows himself unjust in his quarrel does not willingly resign himself to its judgment. So the suspect flees the Real and strives at evasion — for the light of judgment would reveal the secret he hides from himself as much as from others.

versets.ar: وَإِن يَكُن لَّهُمُ الْحَقُّ يَأْتُوا إِلَيْهِ مُذْعِنِينَ
versets.tr: wa-in yakun lahumu l-ḥaqqu yaʾtū ilayhi mudhʿinīna
versets.trad: But if the right is on their side, they come to him submissively.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: they hasten compliant when the right inclines toward their desire, but do not accept the judgment out of faith. They are like the sick man who wavers between health and illness: the people of hypocrisy waver between doubt and knowledge, neither denying by certainty nor affirming by knowledge — tossed about in the valleys of doubt.

versets.ar: أَفِي قُلُوبِهِم مَّرَضٌ أَمِ ارْتَابُوا أَمْ يَخَافُونَ أَن يَحِيفَ اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِمْ وَرَسُولُهُ ۚ بَلْ أُولَٰئِكَ هُمُ الظَّالِمُونَ
versets.tr: a-fī qulūbihim maraḍun ami rtābū am yakhāfūna an yaḥīfa llāhu ʿalayhim wa-rasūluhu bal ulāʾika humu l-ẓālimūna
versets.trad: Is there a sickness in their hearts? Or do they doubt? Or do they fear that God and His Messenger will deal unjustly with them? Nay, those are the wrongdoers!
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: having set out on the thread of conjecture, they arrived only at the darknesses of doubt; and having no certainty in the heart, they had no mention among the people of hearts. Doubt is their dwelling: neither firm denial nor learned affirmation — the injustice they ascribe to the judgment is but the reflection of their own iniquity.

mots.def: maraḍ: here, corruption of the heart.

mots.sens: that He should be unjust, oppress

versets.ar: إِنَّمَا كَانَ قَوْلَ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ إِذَا دُعُوا إِلَى اللَّهِ وَرَسُولِهِ لِيَحْكُمَ بَيْنَهُمْ أَن يَقُولُوا سَمِعْنَا وَأَطَعْنَا ۚ وَأُولَٰئِكَ هُمُ الْمُفْلِحُونَ
versets.tr: innamā kāna qawla l-muʾminīna idhā duʿū ilā llāhi wa-rasūlihi li-yaḥkuma baynahum an yaqūlū samiʿnā wa-aṭaʿnā wa-ulāʾika humu l-mufliḥūna
versets.trad: The only saying of the believers, when called to God and His Messenger that he may judge between them, is to say: “We hear and we obey.” And those are the prosperous.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: those whose faith is truth by the effect of belief have as their mark the manifesting of what they hold of realization; whoever receives God's command with obedience and meets His judgment with humble submission — those are the truthful in reality, the wayfarers on the Path, those who hold fast to the pact.

mots.racine: س م ع / ط و ع

versets.ar: وَمَن يُطِعِ اللَّهَ وَرَسُولَهُ وَيَخْشَ اللَّهَ وَيَتَّقْهِ فَأُولَٰئِكَ هُمُ الْفَائِزُونَ
versets.tr: wa-man yuṭiʿi llāha wa-rasūlahu wa-yakhsha llāha wa-yattaqhi fa-ulāʾika humu l-fāʾizūna
versets.trad: Whoever obeys God and His Messenger, and fears God and is mindful of Him — those are the triumphant.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī makes obedience united with reverential awe (khashya) and watchful piety (taqwā) the mark of the truly triumphant: whoever meets God's command with docility and receives His decree with humility — that is the winner of both abodes. The fear here is no barren dread, but the reverence that keeps the heart turned toward its Lord.

versets.ar: وَأَقْسَمُوا بِاللَّهِ جَهْدَ أَيْمَانِهِمْ لَئِنْ أَمَرْتَهُمْ لَيَخْرُجُنَّ ۖ قُل لَّا تُقْسِمُوا ۖ طَاعَةٌ مَّعْرُوفَةٌ ۚ إِنَّ اللَّهَ خَبِيرٌ بِمَا تَعْمَلُونَ
versets.tr: wa-aqsamū bi-llāhi jahda aymānihim la-in amartahum la-yakhrujunna qul lā tuqsimū ṭāʿatun maʿrūfatun inna llāha khabīrun bimā taʿmalūna
versets.trad: They swear by God their most solemn oaths that if you commanded them, they would march forth. Say: “Do not swear! Obedience is what is fitting. Truly God is well aware of what you do.”
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: they have sworn by God the strongest of oaths and promised future obedience; so it was said to them: do not promise what is already known you will not fulfill — obedience in the moment is better than a promise deferred. The oath that anticipates the act is no substitute for present docility.

mots.racine: ج ه د / ي م ن
ط و ع / ع ر ف

versets.ar: قُلْ أَطِيعُوا اللَّهَ وَأَطِيعُوا الرَّسُولَ ۖ فَإِن تَوَلَّوْا فَإِنَّمَا عَلَيْهِ مَا حُمِّلَ وَعَلَيْكُم مَّا حُمِّلْتُمْ ۖ وَإِن تُطِيعُوهُ تَهْتَدُوا ۚ وَمَا عَلَى الرَّسُولِ إِلَّا الْبَلَاغُ الْمُبِينُ
versets.tr: qul aṭīʿū llāha wa-aṭīʿū l-rasūla fa-in tawallaw fa-innamā ʿalayhi mā ḥummila wa-ʿalaykum mā ḥummiltum wa-in tuṭīʿūhu tahtadū wa-mā ʿalā l-rasūli illā l-balāghu l-mubīnu
versets.trad: Say: “Obey God and obey the Messenger!” If you turn away, upon him is only what he was charged with, and upon you what you were charged with. If you obey him, you will be guided; the Messenger's duty is only to convey the Message clearly.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: if they respond, they will be happy in both abodes and good to themselves; if they turn away, they harm only themselves, and the remorse will be theirs later. Upon the messengers is only the beautiful conveyance; on the Day of Gathering each receives his book and is dealt with according to the reckoning of his own soul.

mots.racine: ب ل غ / ب ي ن

versets.ar: وَعَدَ اللَّهُ الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا مِنكُمْ وَعَمِلُوا الصَّالِحَاتِ لَيَسْتَخْلِفَنَّهُمْ فِي الْأَرْضِ كَمَا اسْتَخْلَفَ الَّذِينَ مِن قَبْلِهِمْ وَلَيُمَكِّنَنَّ لَهُمْ دِينَهُمُ الَّذِي ارْتَضَىٰ لَهُمْ وَلَيُبَدِّلَنَّهُم مِّن بَعْدِ خَوْفِهِمْ أَمْنًا ۚ يَعْبُدُونَنِي لَا يُشْرِكُونَ بِي شَيْئًا ۚ وَمَن كَفَرَ بَعْدَ ذَٰلِكَ فَأُولَٰئِكَ هُمُ الْفَاسِقُونَ
versets.tr: waʿada llāhu lladhīna āmanū minkum wa-ʿamilū l-ṣāliḥāti la-yastakhlifannahum fī l-arḍi kamā stakhlafa lladhīna min qablihim wa-la-yumakkinanna lahum dīnahumu lladhī rtaḍā lahum wa-la-yubaddilannahum min baʿdi khawfihim amnan yaʿbudūnanī lā yushrikūna bī shayʾan wa-man kafara baʿda dhālika fa-ulāʾika humu l-fāsiqūna
versets.trad: God has promised those of you who believe and do righteous deeds that He will surely make them successors on the earth, as He made successors those before them; and He will surely establish for them their religion, which He has approved for them; and He will surely change their fear into security. They worship Me, associating nothing with Me. And whoever disbelieves after that — those are the depraved.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: God's promise is truth, His word is true. He sees in the verse the sign of the imams of religion, pillars of the community and supports of Islam, sincere counselors of the servants, guides for whoever seeks right direction in God — for the harm of unjust rulers is confined to the affairs of this world, while the true guardians of faith are the imams among the learned: keepers of the Qurʾān and ḥadīth, theologians who refute error with the shining proof, jurists to whom one returns, and the people of gnosis and the realities, who are in religion like the intimates of the king.

mots.sens: He will surely make them successors

mots.def: makkana: to make firm, give footing.

versets.ar: وَأَقِيمُوا الصَّلَاةَ وَآتُوا الزَّكَاةَ وَأَطِيعُوا الرَّسُولَ لَعَلَّكُمْ تُرْحَمُونَ
versets.tr: wa-aqīmū l-ṣalāta wa-ātū l-zakāta wa-aṭīʿū l-rasūla laʿallakum turḥamūna
versets.trad: Perform the prayer, give the alms, and obey the Messenger, that you may receive mercy.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī makes this threefold command — prayer, alms, obedience — the way that opens onto mercy: the worship of the body, the sharing of goods, submission to the guide. The divine “perhaps” is no uncertainty, but an invitation to hope: God ties grace to sincere effort, not to magnify Himself, but that the servant may profit thereby.

versets.ar: لَا تَحْسَبَنَّ الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا مُعْجِزِينَ فِي الْأَرْضِ ۚ وَمَأْوَاهُمُ النَّارُ ۖ وَلَبِئْسَ الْمَصِيرُ
versets.tr: lā taḥsabanna lladhīna kafarū muʿjizīna fī l-arḍi wa-maʾwāhumu l-nāru wa-la-biʾsa l-maṣīru
versets.trad: Do not suppose that those who disbelieve can thwart God on the earth. Their refuge is the Fire — and what a wretched destination!
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: the false may have a reign, but it is only illusion without endurance, briefer than a gust born of wrath. The disbelievers cannot bring God to defeat on His earth; their refuge is the Fire — a baleful end, where the fleeting glitter of falsehood comes to nothing.

versets.ar: يَا أَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا لِيَسْتَأْذِنكُمُ الَّذِينَ مَلَكَتْ أَيْمَانُكُمْ وَالَّذِينَ لَمْ يَبْلُغُوا الْحُلُمَ مِنكُمْ ثَلَاثَ مَرَّاتٍ ۚ مِّن قَبْلِ صَلَاةِ الْفَجْرِ وَحِينَ تَضَعُونَ ثِيَابَكُم مِّنَ الظَّهِيرَةِ وَمِن بَعْدِ صَلَاةِ الْعِشَاءِ ۚ ثَلَاثُ عَوْرَاتٍ لَّكُمْ ۚ لَيْسَ عَلَيْكُمْ وَلَا عَلَيْهِمْ جُنَاحٌ بَعْدَهُنَّ ۚ طَوَّافُونَ عَلَيْكُم بَعْضُكُمْ عَلَىٰ بَعْضٍ ۚ كَذَٰلِكَ يُبَيِّنُ اللَّهُ لَكُمُ الْآيَاتِ ۗ وَاللَّهُ عَلِيمٌ حَكِيمٌ
versets.tr: yā ayyuhā lladhīna āmanū li-yastaʾdhinkumu lladhīna malakat aymānukum wa-lladhīna lam yablughū l-ḥuluma minkum thalātha marrātin min qabli ṣalāti l-fajri wa-ḥīna taḍaʿūna thiyābakum mina l-ẓahīrati wa-min baʿdi ṣalāti l-ʿishāʾi thalāthu ʿawrātin lakum laysa ʿalaykum wa-lā ʿalayhim junāḥun baʿdahunna ṭawwāfūna ʿalaykum baʿḍukum ʿalā baʿḍin kadhālika yubayyinu llāhu lakumu l-āyāti wa-llāhu ʿalīmun ḥakīmun
versets.trad: O you who believe! Let those whom your right hands possess, and those of you who have not reached puberty, ask your permission at three times: before the dawn prayer, when you lay aside your garments in the heat of noon, and after the night prayer — three times of privacy for you. Beyond these there is no blame on you or on them: they move about you, some of you with others. Thus God makes clear to you the signs. And God is all-knowing, wise.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: God has narrowed the matter on one side and widened it on another; He has commanded prudence and beautiful conduct in upholding the rules of faith, respecting intimacy, and being freed from the perils of temptation. And he closes with a spiritual maxim: when the approaches are guarded, fears become safety.

mots.def: ʿawra: that which is to be covered, intimacy.

mots.sens: those who move about, come and go

versets.ar: وَإِذَا بَلَغَ الْأَطْفَالُ مِنكُمُ الْحُلُمَ فَلْيَسْتَأْذِنُوا كَمَا اسْتَأْذَنَ الَّذِينَ مِن قَبْلِهِمْ ۚ كَذَٰلِكَ يُبَيِّنُ اللَّهُ لَكُمْ آيَاتِهِ ۗ وَاللَّهُ عَلِيمٌ حَكِيمٌ
versets.tr: wa-idhā balagha l-aṭfālu minkumu l-ḥuluma fa-l-yastaʾdhinū kamā staʾdhana lladhīna min qablihim kadhālika yubayyinu llāhu lakum āyātihi wa-llāhu ʿalīmun ḥakīmun
versets.trad: And when your children reach puberty, let them ask permission, as those before them asked. Thus God makes clear to you His signs. And God is all-knowing, wise.
versets.commentaire: Once puberty is reached, the child enters the common rule of permission, with no exception of the three times. Qushayrī ties this gradation to God's knowledge and wisdom: He makes His signs clear by degrees, measuring to each age the charge that befits it, so that the education of modesty may take root in childhood and be fulfilled at maturity.

versets.ar: وَالْقَوَاعِدُ مِنَ النِّسَاءِ اللَّاتِي لَا يَرْجُونَ نِكَاحًا فَلَيْسَ عَلَيْهِنَّ جُنَاحٌ أَن يَضَعْنَ ثِيَابَهُنَّ غَيْرَ مُتَبَرِّجَاتٍ بِزِينَةٍ ۖ وَأَن يَسْتَعْفِفْنَ خَيْرٌ لَّهُنَّ ۗ وَاللَّهُ سَمِيعٌ عَلِيمٌ
versets.tr: wa-l-qawāʿidu mina l-nisāʾi llātī lā yarjūna nikāḥan fa-laysa ʿalayhinna junāḥun an yaḍaʿna thiyābahunna ghayra mutabarrijātin bi-zīnatin wa-an yastaʿfifna khayrun lahunna wa-llāhu samīʿun ʿalīmun
versets.trad: As for women past child-bearing who no longer hope for marriage, there is no blame on them if they lay aside their outer garments, without displaying their adornment; but to remain modest is better for them. And God is all-hearing, all-knowing.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: the harm to the secrets of the inmost self comes from the calls of temptation and the dominion of desire; but when that turmoil subsides, the door opens, the dispensations become lawful, and one is safe from sedition. So the leniency granted to aged women follows the extinction of the trouble they might stir.

mots.def: qāʿid: an aged woman who no longer bears children.
mutabarrija: one who displays herself with finery.
istaʿaffa: to keep oneself, stay chaste.

versets.ar: لَّيْسَ عَلَى الْأَعْمَىٰ حَرَجٌ وَلَا عَلَى الْأَعْرَجِ حَرَجٌ وَلَا عَلَى الْمَرِيضِ حَرَجٌ وَلَا عَلَىٰ أَنفُسِكُمْ أَن تَأْكُلُوا مِن بُيُوتِكُمْ أَوْ بُيُوتِ آبَائِكُمْ أَوْ بُيُوتِ أُمَّهَاتِكُمْ أَوْ بُيُوتِ إِخْوَانِكُمْ أَوْ بُيُوتِ أَخَوَاتِكُمْ أَوْ بُيُوتِ أَعْمَامِكُمْ أَوْ بُيُوتِ عَمَّاتِكُمْ أَوْ بُيُوتِ أَخْوَالِكُمْ أَوْ بُيُوتِ خَالَاتِكُمْ أَوْ مَا مَلَكْتُم مَّفَاتِحَهُ أَوْ صَدِيقِكُمْ ۚ لَيْسَ عَلَيْكُمْ جُنَاحٌ أَن تَأْكُلُوا جَمِيعًا أَوْ أَشْتَاتًا ۚ فَإِذَا دَخَلْتُم بُيُوتًا فَسَلِّمُوا عَلَىٰ أَنفُسِكُمْ تَحِيَّةً مِّنْ عِندِ اللَّهِ مُبَارَكَةً طَيِّبَةً ۚ كَذَٰلِكَ يُبَيِّنُ اللَّهُ لَكُمُ الْآيَاتِ لَعَلَّكُمْ تَعْقِلُونَ
versets.tr: laysa ʿalā l-aʿmā ḥarajun wa-lā ʿalā l-aʿraji ḥarajun wa-lā ʿalā l-marīḍi ḥarajun wa-lā ʿalā anfusikum an taʾkulū min buyūtikum aw buyūti ābāʾikum aw buyūti ummahātikum aw buyūti ikhwānikum aw buyūti akhawātikum aw buyūti aʿmāmikum aw buyūti ʿammātikum aw buyūti akhwālikum aw buyūti khālātikum aw mā malaktum mafātiḥahu aw ṣadīqikum laysa ʿalaykum junāḥun an taʾkulū jamīʿan aw ashtātan fa-idhā dakhaltum buyūtan fa-sallimū ʿalā anfusikum taḥiyyatan min ʿindi llāhi mubārakatan ṭayyibatan kadhālika yubayyinu llāhu lakumu l-āyāti laʿallakum taʿqilūna
versets.trad: There is no blame on the blind, nor on the lame, nor on the sick, nor on yourselves, if you eat in your own houses, or the houses of your fathers, or your mothers, or your brothers, or your sisters, or your paternal uncles, or your paternal aunts, or your maternal uncles, or your maternal aunts, or those of which you hold the keys, or of your friend. There is no blame on you whether you eat together or separately. But when you enter houses, greet one another with a greeting from God, blessed and good. Thus God makes clear to you the signs, that you may understand.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: when excuses come, the trial is eased; when kinship is established, constraint falls away; and when nearness is true, the distinction of the stranger vanishes. On “or your friend” he softens: rare is one sincere in friendship, being within as he appears without, not a mirror before you and shears behind your back. On the greeting: the greeting is safekeeping; the believer's way, on entering a house, is to ask of God, for his own soul, peace and preservation from all that would displease Him, for it is not lawful for the believer to slacken for an instant from seeking refuge in God.

mots.racine: ح ي ي / ب ر ك

versets.ar: إِنَّمَا الْمُؤْمِنُونَ الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا بِاللَّهِ وَرَسُولِهِ وَإِذَا كَانُوا مَعَهُ عَلَىٰ أَمْرٍ جَامِعٍ لَّمْ يَذْهَبُوا حَتَّىٰ يَسْتَأْذِنُوهُ ۚ إِنَّ الَّذِينَ يَسْتَأْذِنُونَكَ أُولَٰئِكَ الَّذِينَ يُؤْمِنُونَ بِاللَّهِ وَرَسُولِهِ ۚ فَإِذَا اسْتَأْذَنُوكَ لِبَعْضِ شَأْنِهِمْ فَأْذَن لِّمَن شِئْتَ مِنْهُمْ وَاسْتَغْفِرْ لَهُمُ اللَّهَ ۚ إِنَّ اللَّهَ غَفُورٌ رَّحِيمٌ
versets.tr: innamā l-muʾminūna lladhīna āmanū bi-llāhi wa-rasūlihi wa-idhā kānū maʿahu ʿalā amrin jāmiʿin lam yadhhabū ḥattā yastaʾdhinūhu inna lladhīna yastaʾdhinūnaka ulāʾika lladhīna yuʾminūna bi-llāhi wa-rasūlihi fa-idhā staʾdhanūka li-baʿḍi shaʾnihim fa-ʾdhan li-man shiʾta minhum wa-staghfir lahumu llāha inna llāha ghafūrun raḥīmun
versets.trad: The believers are only those who believe in God and His Messenger, and who, when they are with him on a matter of common concern, do not depart until they have asked his leave. Those who ask your leave are indeed those who believe in God and His Messenger. So when they ask your leave for some affair of their own, give leave to whom you will of them, and ask forgiveness of God for them. Truly God is forgiving, compassionate.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī makes this verse the code of the disciple: the condition of following is accord with the one followed, and not to scatter into factions, as in “you suppose them united, but their hearts are divided.” The learned are the heirs of the prophets, and the aspirants to their shaykhs are as the community to its prophet: the disciple must not draw a breath without his shaykh's permission. Whoever contradicts him soon sees its reverse; and if he has erred, let him hasten to excuse himself and confess his lapse.

mots.racine: ء م ر / ج م ع

versets.ar: لَّا تَجْعَلُوا دُعَاءَ الرَّسُولِ بَيْنَكُمْ كَدُعَاءِ بَعْضِكُم بَعْضًا ۚ قَدْ يَعْلَمُ اللَّهُ الَّذِينَ يَتَسَلَّلُونَ مِنكُمْ لِوَاذًا ۚ فَلْيَحْذَرِ الَّذِينَ يُخَالِفُونَ عَنْ أَمْرِهِ أَن تُصِيبَهُمْ فِتْنَةٌ أَوْ يُصِيبَهُمْ عَذَابٌ أَلِيمٌ
versets.tr: lā tajʿalū duʿāʾa l-rasūli baynakum ka-duʿāʾi baʿḍikum baʿḍan qad yaʿlamu llāhu lladhīna yatasallalūna minkum liwādhan fa-l-yaḥdhari lladhīna yukhālifūna ʿan amrihi an tuṣībahum fitnatun aw yuṣībahum ʿadhābun alīmun
versets.trad: Do not treat the Messenger's summons among you as you treat one another's calling. God knows those of you who slip away under cover. Let those who go against his command beware lest a trial befall them, or a painful punishment befall them.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: magnify him in address, observe the adab in his service, embrace his obedience with awe and veneration. And on the warning: the felicity of both worlds is in following the Sunna, and the misery of the fallen in abandoning it; the least harm that strikes one who departs from it is the loss of accord, the impossibility of following thereafter, and the fall of all dignity from his heart.

versets.ar: أَلَا إِنَّ لِلَّهِ مَا فِي السَّمَاوَاتِ وَالْأَرْضِ ۖ قَدْ يَعْلَمُ مَا أَنتُمْ عَلَيْهِ وَيَوْمَ يُرْجَعُونَ إِلَيْهِ فَيُنَبِّئُهُم بِمَا عَمِلُوا ۗ وَاللَّهُ بِكُلِّ شَيْءٍ عَلِيمٌ
versets.tr: a-lā inna li-llāhi mā fī l-samāwāti wa-l-arḍi qad yaʿlamu mā antum ʿalayhi wa-yawma yurjaʿūna ilayhi fa-yunabbiʾuhum bimā ʿamilū wa-llāhu bi-kulli shayʾin ʿalīmun
versets.trad: Behold, to God belongs all that is in the heavens and the earth. He knows the state you are in, and the Day when they will be returned to Him, then He will inform them of what they did. And God is the Knower of all things.
versets.commentaire: The seal of the sūra. Qushayrī: the day has its morrow, and the servant's act has its reckoning: the responsible one will be questioned about the great and the small, the slightest and the most tenuous. All, in the heavens and the earth, belongs to God; nothing escapes His knowledge, neither the present state, nor the Day of return when He will unveil the deeds.
